name: Sumo Deadlift
instructions_text: Stand with wide stance and toes turned out. Grip bar between legs with narrow grip. Drive through heels to stand. Keep chest proud throughout. Easier on lower back than conventional.

name: Reverse Lunge
instructions_text: Stand tall. Step one foot back and lower back knee toward ground. Both knees at 90 degrees. Push through front heel to return to standing. Easier on knees than forward lunges.

name: Skater Jumps
instructions_text: Stand on one foot. Jump laterally to the other foot, landing softly with a slight knee bend. Swing arms for balance. Immediately jump back to the other side. Like speed skating.

name: Goblet Squat
instructions_text: Hold a dumbbell vertically at chest height with both hands cupping the top end. Stand with feet slightly wider than shoulder-width. Squat down keeping elbows inside your knees and chest tall. Push through your heels to return to standing.

name: Dumbbell Bench Press
instructions_text: Lie on a flat bench holding a dumbbell in each hand at chest level. Press both dumbbells up until arms are extended. Lower with control back to the starting position. Keep a slight arch in your lower back and feet planted firmly.

name: Single-Leg Glute Bridge
instructions_text: Lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat on the floor. Extend one leg straight out. Push through the heel of your grounded foot to lift your hips until your body forms a straight line from shoulders to knee. Lower with control and repeat. Switch legs after completing all reps.

name: Chest-Supported Dumbbell Row
instructions_text: Set an incline bench to about 30-45 degrees. Lie face down on the bench with a dumbbell in each hand hanging below. Row both dumbbells up by squeezing your shoulder blades together. Lower with control. This variation removes lower back strain from the movement.

name: Shrugs (Dumbbell)
instructions_text: Stand holding heavy dumbbells at sides. Shrug shoulders straight up toward ears. Hold briefly at top. Lower with control. Avoid rolling shoulders.

name: Pigeon Stretch
instructions_text: From all fours, bring one knee forward behind your wrist with shin angled. Extend other leg straight back. Lower hips toward floor. Hold for prescribed time. Deep hip opener. Note: reps = seconds.

name: Wrist Circles
instructions_text: Extend arms forward. Make slow circles with your wrists — clockwise then counter-clockwise. Do 10-15 each direction. Good warm-up before pressing exercises.

name: Barbell Row
instructions_text: Hinge forward at the hips with a slight bend in your knees, holding the bar with an overhand grip. Pull the bar to your lower chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together at the top. Lower with control. Keep your back flat and avoid using momentum.

name: Dead Bug
instructions_text: Lie on your back with arms extended toward the ceiling and knees bent at 90 degrees above your hips. Slowly extend one arm overhead while extending the opposite leg, keeping your lower back pressed into the floor. Return to start and alternate sides. Each side counts as one rep.

name: Cat-Cow Stretch
instructions_text: Start on hands and knees with wrists under shoulders and knees under hips. For cow: drop your belly, lift your chest and tailbone. For cat: round your back, tuck your chin and tailbone. Flow smoothly between the two positions with your breath — inhale for cow, exhale for cat.

name: Incline Dumbbell Press
instructions_text: Set bench to 30-45 degrees. Press dumbbells from shoulder height to full extension above upper chest. Lower with control. Keeps emphasis on upper chest.

name: Rear Delt Fly (Dumbbell)
instructions_text: Hinge forward at hips holding light dumbbells. Raise arms out to sides squeezing shoulder blades together. Lower with control. Keep slight bend in elbows throughout.

name: Tricep Dips (Bench)
instructions_text: Sit on the edge of a bench with hands gripping the edge beside your hips. Slide your hips off the bench with legs extended (or bent for easier version). Lower your body by bending elbows to about 90 degrees. Push back up to straight arms. Keep your back close to the bench.

name: Hip Flexor Stretch (Half-Kneeling)
instructions_text: Kneel on one knee with the other foot flat in front (90 degree angle at both knees). Tuck your tailbone slightly and lean your hips gently forward until you feel a stretch in the front of your back hip. Hold for the prescribed time, breathing deeply. Switch sides. Note: rep numbers represent seconds.

name: Seated Cable Row
instructions_text: Sit at the cable row machine with feet on the platform and knees slightly bent. Grasp the handle with arms extended. Pull the handle to your stomach while squeezing shoulder blades together. Keep your back upright — avoid leaning back to pull. Return with control.

name: Glute Bridge
instructions_text: Lie on back with knees bent and feet flat. Push through heels to lift hips until body forms straight line from shoulders to knees. Squeeze glutes at top. Lower with control.

name: Leg Extension (Machine)
instructions_text: Sit in leg extension machine with shins behind pad. Extend legs fully by straightening knees. Squeeze quads at top. Lower with control.

name: Box Squat
instructions_text: Set a box or bench behind you at knee height. Squat back onto the box with control, pause briefly, then drive up. Teaches proper depth and hip mechanics.

name: Dumbbell Lateral Lunge
instructions_text: Hold dumbbells at sides. Step wide to one side, bending that knee and pushing hips back while keeping other leg straight. Push back to start. Alternate sides.

name: Upright Row (Dumbbell)
instructions_text: Hold dumbbells in front of thighs. Pull them straight up along your body, leading with elbows until they reach shoulder height. Lower with control. Keep the weight close to your body.

name: Overhead Press (Barbell)
instructions_text: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, holding the bar at shoulder height with hands just outside shoulders. Brace your core and press the bar overhead until arms are fully extended. Lower the bar back to shoulders with control. Avoid arching your lower back excessively.

name: Dumbbell Row
instructions_text: Place one knee and hand on a bench for support. Hold a dumbbell in the other hand with arm extended. Pull the dumbbell to your hip, leading with your elbow. Squeeze your shoulder blade at the top, then lower with control. Complete all reps on one side, then switch.

name: Bulgarian Split Squat
instructions_text: Stand about two feet in front of a bench. Place the top of one foot on the bench behind you. Lower your body until your front thigh is parallel to the ground. Push through your front heel to return to standing. Keep your torso upright and front knee tracking over your toes.

name: Jumping Jacks
instructions_text: Stand with feet together and arms at your sides. Jump your feet out wide while raising your arms overhead. Jump back to the starting position. Keep a soft bend in your knees when landing. Maintain a steady, rhythmic pace.

name: Seated Neck Stretch
instructions_text: Sit tall. Gently tilt your head toward one shoulder until you feel a stretch on the opposite side of your neck. You can lightly rest your hand on your head for gentle added stretch — never pull. Hold for the prescribed time, then switch sides. Note: rep numbers represent seconds per side.

name: Bodyweight Squat
instructions_text: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, toes slightly turned out. Extend arms forward for balance. Lower your hips back and down as if sitting into a chair. Go as deep as comfortable while keeping your heels on the floor. Stand back up by pushing through your whole foot.

name: Banded Squat
instructions_text: Stand on band with feet shoulder-width. Hold band at shoulders. Squat down and stand back up. Band adds resistance at the top of the movement. Keep chest up.

name: Resistance Band Pull-Apart
instructions_text: Hold a resistance band with both hands at shoulder height, arms extended in front of you. Pull the band apart by squeezing your shoulder blades together until your arms are out to your sides. Return slowly to the starting position. Keep a slight bend in your elbows.

name: Mountain Climbers
instructions_text: Start in a push-up position with arms straight. Drive one knee toward your chest, then quickly switch legs. Continue alternating at a quick pace. Keep your hips level and core engaged throughout. Each leg drive counts as one rep.

name: Bear Crawl
instructions_text: Start on all fours with knees hovering just above floor. Move forward by stepping opposite hand and foot simultaneously. Keep hips level and core braced. Move slowly with control.

name: Chest Stretch (Doorway)
instructions_text: Stand in a doorway with arm bent at 90 degrees against the frame at shoulder height. Lean forward gently until you feel a stretch across your chest. Hold. Switch sides. Note: reps = seconds per side.

name: Barbell Bench Press
instructions_text: Lie on a flat bench with eyes under the bar. Grip the bar slightly wider than shoulder-width. Unrack and lower the bar to mid-chest with control. Press the bar back up to full arm extension. Keep shoulder blades pinched together and feet flat on the floor throughout.

name: Walking Lunges
instructions_text: Stand tall, take a large step forward and lower your back knee toward the ground. Both knees should bend to about 90 degrees. Push off your front foot and step the back leg forward into the next lunge. Keep your torso upright throughout.

name: Bicycle Crunch
instructions_text: Lie on back with hands behind head. Lift shoulders off floor. Bring one knee toward chest while rotating opposite elbow toward it. Alternate sides in a pedaling motion.

name: Kettlebell Swing
instructions_text: Stand with feet wider than hip-width, kettlebell on the floor between your feet. Hinge at hips to grip the kettlebell. Hike it back between your legs, then explosively drive your hips forward to swing the kettlebell to chest height. Let it swing back and repeat. Power comes from your hips, not your arms.

name: Hammer Curl
instructions_text: Hold dumbbells with palms facing each other (neutral grip). Curl up keeping palms inward throughout. Lower with control. Targets brachialis and forearms.

name: Leg Curl (Machine)
instructions_text: Lie face down on leg curl machine with pad on back of ankles. Curl legs up by bending knees. Squeeze hamstrings at top. Lower slowly.

name: Step-Ups
instructions_text: Stand facing a bench or sturdy platform. Step up with one foot, drive through that heel to stand on top. Step back down. Alternate legs or complete one side first.

name: Inchworm
instructions_text: Stand tall. Hinge forward and walk hands out to push-up position. Optionally do a push-up. Walk hands back to feet and stand. Great warm-up combining hamstring stretch and core work.

name: Shoulder Pass-Through
instructions_text: Hold a band or stick with wide overhand grip. Keeping arms straight, raise it overhead and behind your back in one smooth arc. Return the same way. Go only as far as comfortable. Improves shoulder mobility.

name: Lat Pulldown
instructions_text: Sit at a lat pulldown machine with thighs secured under the pads. Grip the bar wider than shoulder-width. Pull the bar down to your upper chest while squeezing your shoulder blades together. Return the bar up with control. Avoid leaning too far back.

name: Plank
instructions_text: Support yourself on your forearms and toes with your body in a straight line from head to heels. Keep your core braced and hips level — don't let them sag or pike up. Breathe steadily. Hold for the prescribed time. Note: rep numbers represent seconds for this exercise.

name: Lateral Raises (Dumbbell)
instructions_text: Stand holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides. Raise your arms out to the sides until they reach shoulder height, keeping a slight bend in your elbows. Slowly lower back down. Avoid shrugging your shoulders up — think about pushing the dumbbells away from you.

name: Wall Sit
instructions_text: Lean your back flat against a wall and slide down until your thighs are parallel to the ground (or as low as comfortable). Your knees should be at 90 degrees directly over your ankles. Hold this position for the prescribed time. Note: rep numbers represent seconds.

name: Arnold Press
instructions_text: Start with dumbbells at shoulder height, palms facing you. As you press up, rotate palms to face forward at the top. Reverse the rotation as you lower. Full range of motion for all deltoid heads.

name: Farmer Walk
instructions_text: Hold heavy dumbbells at sides. Walk forward with tall posture, shoulders back, core braced. Maintain even pace. Great for grip strength and overall stability.

name: Hanging Leg Raise
instructions_text: Hang from a bar with straight arms. Raise legs (bent or straight) until at least parallel to ground. Lower with control. Avoid swinging.

name: Romanian Deadlift (Dumbbell)
instructions_text: Stand holding dumbbells in front of your thighs with a slight bend in your knees. Hinge at your hips, pushing them back while lowering the dumbbells along your legs. Keep your back flat and feel a stretch in your hamstrings. Return to standing by squeezing your glutes at the top.

name: Pull-Up
instructions_text: Hang from a bar with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width, palms facing away. Pull yourself up until your chin clears the bar. Lower with control to a full hang. Avoid swinging or using momentum. If too difficult, use a resistance band for assistance.

name: Dumbbell Bicep Curl
instructions_text: Stand holding a dumbbell in each hand with arms at your sides, palms facing forward. Curl the dumbbells up toward your shoulders by bending at the elbows. Squeeze at the top, then lower with control. Keep your elbows pinned to your sides — avoid swinging.

name: Diamond Push-Up
instructions_text: Get into push-up position with hands close together, thumbs and index fingers forming a diamond shape. Lower chest to hands. Push back up. Harder than regular push-ups.

name: Band Pull-Through
instructions_text: Anchor band low behind you. Straddle band facing away. Hinge at hips letting band pull hands back between legs. Drive hips forward to stand tall squeezing glutes. Great hip hinge learning tool.

name: Face Pull (Band)
instructions_text: Anchor a resistance band at face height. Grip both ends and step back to create tension. Pull the band toward your face, separating your hands and squeezing your shoulder blades together. Your hands should end up beside your ears. Return slowly to start.

name: Calf Raises
instructions_text: Stand with feet hip-width apart, optionally on the edge of a step for extra range. Rise up onto your toes as high as possible, squeezing your calves at the top. Lower slowly back to the starting position. Hold onto something for balance if needed.

name: Front Squat
instructions_text: Rest barbell on front of shoulders with elbows high. Squat down keeping torso very upright. Requires good wrist and shoulder mobility. More quad-dominant than back squat.

name: Back Extension (45 degree)
instructions_text: Position yourself in a 45-degree back extension station with hips on pad. Cross arms over chest. Lower torso toward floor. Raise back up until body is straight. Do not hyperextend.

name: Banded Row
instructions_text: Sit with legs extended, band looped around feet. Pull band toward your stomach squeezing shoulder blades together. Return with control. Keep back straight throughout.

name: Barbell Back Squat
instructions_text: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, bar resting on upper traps. Brace your core, push hips back and bend knees to lower until thighs are parallel to the floor. Drive through your heels to stand back up. Keep chest up and knees tracking over toes throughout.

name: Pike Push-Up
instructions_text: Start in a downward dog position with hips high and hands shoulder-width apart. Bend your elbows to lower the top of your head toward the floor. Push back up to the starting position. The more vertical your torso, the harder it is on your shoulders.

name: Burpees
instructions_text: From standing, squat down and place hands on the floor. Jump your feet back to a push-up position. Perform a push-up (optional), then jump feet back to your hands. Explode upward into a jump with arms overhead. Land softly and immediately go into the next rep.

name: Leg Press
instructions_text: Sit in the leg press machine with feet shoulder-width apart on the platform. Release the safety and lower the platform by bending your knees toward your chest. Press back up until legs are almost straight — don't lock out your knees. Keep your lower back pressed into the seat.

name: Skull Crushers
instructions_text: Lie on bench holding barbell with narrow grip above forehead. Bend elbows to lower bar toward forehead. Extend back up. Keep elbows pointing at ceiling throughout.

name: Ankle Circles
instructions_text: Stand on one foot (hold something for balance). Lift other foot and draw slow circles with your toes — clockwise then counter-clockwise. 10-15 each direction per foot.

name: Push-Up
instructions_text: Start in a plank position with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width. Lower your body until your chest nearly touches the floor. Keep your body in a straight line from head to heels. Push back up to the starting position. If full push-ups are too difficult, start with knees on the ground.

name: World's Greatest Stretch
instructions_text: Step into a deep lunge. Place the hand on the same side as your front foot on the ground inside your foot. Rotate your other arm up toward the ceiling, opening your chest. Hold for a breath, then switch sides. Each side counts as one rep.

name: Cable Chest Fly
instructions_text: Stand between cable stacks with handles at chest height. Step forward. Bring handles together in front of chest with slight elbow bend. Squeeze at center, return with control.

name: Tricep Pushdown (Cable)
instructions_text: Stand facing cable machine with rope or bar attachment at top. Push down by extending elbows until arms are straight. Keep elbows pinned to sides. Return with control.

name: Hip Thrust (Barbell)
instructions_text: Sit with upper back against a bench, barbell across hips. Drive through heels to lift hips until body forms straight line from shoulders to knees. Squeeze glutes at top. Lower with control.

name: Russian Twist
instructions_text: Sit with knees bent, feet off floor, lean back slightly. Rotate torso side to side, touching the ground beside each hip. Keep core engaged throughout. Can hold a weight for added challenge.

name: Barbell Deadlift
instructions_text: Stand with feet hip-width apart, bar over mid-foot. Hinge at hips and grip the bar just outside your knees. Flatten your back, brace your core, and drive through your feet to stand up. Keep the bar close to your body throughout. Reverse the movement to lower the bar back down with control.

name: High Knees
instructions_text: Stand tall. Run in place driving knees up to hip height with each step. Pump arms. Maintain quick pace. Keep core engaged and land softly.

name: Jump Squats
instructions_text: Start in squat position. Explode upward jumping as high as possible. Land softly bending knees to absorb impact. Immediately go into next rep.

name: Dumbbell Shoulder Press
instructions_text: Sit or stand holding a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height with palms facing forward. Press both dumbbells overhead until arms are fully extended. Lower back to shoulder height with control. Keep your core tight to avoid arching your back.

name: Inverted Row
instructions_text: Set a bar at waist height (or use a sturdy table edge). Hang underneath with arms extended and body straight. Pull your chest to the bar by squeezing your shoulder blades together. Lower with control. The more horizontal your body, the harder it is.

name: Thoracic Rotation (Seated)
instructions_text: Sit on a chair or bench with feet flat. Cross arms over your chest. Keeping hips facing forward, slowly rotate your upper body to one side as far as comfortable. Hold briefly, return to center, then rotate to the other side. Each side counts as one rep. Move slowly and with control.

name: Ab Wheel Rollout
instructions_text: Kneel with an ab wheel (or use a towel on smooth floor). Roll forward extending arms, keeping core tight and back flat. Go as far as you can control. Pull back to start using your abs.

name: Superman Hold
instructions_text: Lie face down with arms extended overhead. Simultaneously lift arms, chest, and legs off the floor. Hold briefly. Lower with control. Strengthens posterior chain.
